title: Financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund
subtitle: The total financial support provided to developing countries for climate change mitigation and adaptation. Countries have set a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards.
caption:
Financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund, 2011 to 2022
The total financial support provided to developing countries for climate change mitigation and adaptation. Countries have set a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards.

This line chart tracks the annual total of financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund from 2011 to 2022, measured in current US dollars. The vertical axis ranges from $0 to $80 billion, with gridlines marking $10 billion increments. The single line labeled "World" begins in 2011 at a value just under $30 billion and remains flat through 2012. A visible rise occurs in 2013, where the line crosses the $40 billion mark. The upward trajectory continues through 2016, reaching a local peak slightly above $50 billion. A dip appears in 2017, bringing the value back below $50 billion, followed by a recovery in 2018 to approximately $55 billion. The line shows a slight decline in 2019 and remains flat through 2020 at a level just above $50 billion. From 2020 to 2022, the chart displays a steep upward slope, with the line crossing $60 billion in 2021 and ending at the series maximum of $80 billion in 2022. The data shows a general increase in funding over the twelve-year period, with the most rapid growth occurring in the final two years. The 2022 value sits at the top edge of the chart's vertical range, representing the highest recorded annual total in the visible timeframe.
